hadiths.chapter: 9. CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR
hadiths.ar:
5064   -  وَّ رَّوَّى إِن ْن ُّ مَّحْن ُّوب ٍ عَّن ْ جَّم َّاذِ ن ْن ِ ر ِي َّاذٍ عَّن ْ سُّلَّي ْمَّان َّ ن ْن ِ ج َّالِذٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس لاَّمُّ : ق ِى  رَّج ُّلٍ 
ق َّالَّ لأِمْزَّإ َّي ِهِ ن َّعْذَّ مَّا ذَّج َّلَّث ْ عَّلَّي ْهِ لَّمْ إ َّج ِذْك ِ عَّذ ْرَّإءَّ ق َّال
hadiths.en:
Al-Alaa narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim from Abu Jafar Imam 
Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} regarding a man who accuses his wife (of adultery).  
Imam {a.s} said: “He is to be flogged."  
I asked: “What if she pardons him?"  
Imam {a.s} said: “No, and no honor (for him)." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.48 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.10 p.80 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.4 
p.232 • Al-Wafi, Vol.15 p.535 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.28 p.207  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 9, H. 5063

hadiths.chapter: 9. CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR
hadiths.en:
Ibn Mahbub narrated from Hammad ibn Ziyad from Sulayman ibn Khalid from 
Abu Abdullah {a.s} regarding a man who said to his wife after consummating the 
marriage, "I did not find you a virgin"  
Imam {a.s} said: “There is no legal punishment upon him." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.48 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.10 p.78 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.4 
p.231 • Al-Wafi, Vol.15 p.366 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.22 p.438  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 9, H. 5064

hadiths.chapter: 9. CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR
hadiths.ar:
5066  -  وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ وَّهْث ِ ن ْن ِ وَّهْث ٍ عَّن ْ ج َّعْق َّزِ ن ْن ِ مُّحَّم َّذٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِي هِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم   إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ لَّمْ ي َّكُّن ْ 
ي َّحُّذ ُّ ق ِى  إَّلي  َّعْزِي ص ِ جَّي  َّى ي َّا ْي ِى َّ ن ِالْق ِزْي َّه ِ إَّلْمُّصَّز َّجَّه ِ مِي ْلِ ي َّا ر َّإن ِ وَّ ي َّا إِن ْن َّ إَّلز  َّإن ِي َّه ِ إ َّوْ لَّسْث َّ لِأ َّن ِي ك
hadiths.en:
In another narration, Imam {a.s} said: "Indeed, the hymen may be lost without 
intercourse; it can be lost due to injury, stumbling, or falling."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.49 • Al -Wafi, Vol.15 p.366 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.22 
p.438  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 9, H. 5065

hadiths.chapter: 9. CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR
hadiths.ar:
5067  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحَّسَّن ُّ ن ْن ُّ مَّحْن ُّوب ٍ عَّن ْ عَّن  َّاذِ ن ْن ِ صُّهَّي ْث ٍ ق َّالَّ: سُّن ِلَّ إ َّن ُّو عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ْ ن َّصْزَّإن ِى ٍّ ق َّذ َّف َّ  
مُّسْلِماً ق َّق َّالَّ لَّهُّ ي َّا ر َّإن ِ ق َّال ذَّة ً إ ِلأ َّ سَّوْطاً لِحُّزْمَّه ِ إَّلْأ ِسْلاَّمِ وَّ ي ُّحْلَّق ُّ 
رَّإ ْسُّهُّ وَّ ي ُّطَّاف ُّ ن ِهِ ق ِى  إ َّهْلِ ذِي ن ِهِ لِكَّى ْ ي ُّن َّك َّلَّ ع َّي ْزُّة
hadiths.en:
In the narration of Wahb ibn Wahb from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq 
{a.s} from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s}:  
"Indeed, Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} would not administer the legal punishment for 
insinuation unless it was a clear and explicit accusation, such as saying, 'O’ adulterer,' 
or 'O’ son of an adulteress,' or 'You are not your father's son.'"  
[REFERENCES] Qurb Al-Isnad, Vol.1 p.54 • Qurb Al -Isnad, Vol.1 p.155 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 
p.49 • Al-Wafi, Vol.15 p.368 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.28 p.205 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.76 p.117  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 9, H. 5066

reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR

hadiths.chapter: 9. CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR
hadiths.ar:
5068  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ صَّق ْوَّإن َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ن َّكْزٍ إَّلْحَّص ْزَّمِى ِّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ: سَّا َّلْي ُّه عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ ي َّق ْي َّزِى   
عَّلَّى رَّج ُّلٍ مِن ْ ج َّاهِلِي  َّه ِ إَّلْعَّزَّب ِ ق َّال  ذ َّلِك َّ ي َّذْج ُّلُّ عَّلَّى رَّسُّولِ إَّلل َّهِ 
صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ
hadiths.en:
Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Abbad ibn Suhayb who said:  
Abu Abdullah {a.s} was asked about a Christian who accused a Muslim by saying to him, 
"O’ adulterer."  
Imam {a.s} said: “He is to be flogged eighty lashes for violating the right of the Muslim 
and eighty lashes minus one for violating the sanctity of Islam. His head is to be 
shaved, and he is to be paraded among the people of his religion so that others may 
be deterred." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.239 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.49 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.10 p.75 
• Al-Wafi, Vol.15 p.380 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.28 p.199  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 9, H. 5067
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩، ح ٥٠٦٧

hadiths.chapter: 9. CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR
hadiths.ar:
5069   -  وَّ رَّوَّى ج َّعْق َّزُّ ن ْن ُّ ن َّس ِي زٍ عَّن ِ إَّلْحُّسَّي ْن ِ ن ْن ِ إ َّن ِى  إَّلْعَّلاَّءِ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  مَّح ْلَّذٍ إَّلس َّز َّإح ِ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إ لل َّهِ عَّ : إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّص َّى 
ق ِى  رَّج ُّلٍ ذَّعَّا إ ج َّزَّ إِن ْن َّ إَّلْمَّح ْن ُّون ِ وَّ ق َّالَّ إَّلْأ ج َّزُّ لَّهُّ ن َّلْ إ َّن ْث َّ إِن ْن ُّ إَّلْمَّح ْن ُّون ِ ق َّ ا َّمَّزَّ إَّلْأ َّو َّلَّ إ َّن ْ ي َّح ْلِذَّ صَّاجِن َّهُّ عِس ْزِي ن َّ ج َّلْذَّة ً  
وَّ ق َّال زِي ن َّ ن َّكَّالأً ي ُّن َّكِّلُّهُّمَّا
hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Safwan from Abu Bakr Al -Hadrami from Imam Abu 
Abdullah {a.s} who said: I asked Imam {a.s} about a man who slanders a man from the pre-
Islamic Arabs.  
Imam {a.s} said: “He is to be given the legal punishment."  
I asked: “Is he to be given the legal punishment?"  
He said: “Yes, because that offense reaches the Messenger of Allah {SWT}." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 4 p.49 • Ilal Al -Shara’i’, Vol. 2 p.393 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, 
Vol.16 p.37 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.16 p.254 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.64 p.174  
 ﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 9, H. 5068

reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR

hadiths.chapter: 9. CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR
hadiths.ar:
5070   -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ِ هِلاَّلٍ عَّن ْ عُّق ْن َّه َّ ن ْن ِ ج َّالِذٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلا مُّ ق َّالَّ: سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ْ 
رَّج ُّلٍ ق َّالَّ لأِمْزَّإ َّي ِهِ ي َّا ر َّإن ِي َّه ُّ ق َّال لِذَّ وَّ لأَّ ي َّكُّون ُّ إِمْزَّإ َّي َّه 
كَّلاَّماً إ َّق ْلَّث َّ مِن ْهُّ ق ِى  ع َّي ْزِ إ َّن ْ ي َّعْلَّمَّ س َّي ْن اً إ َّرَّإذَّ إ َّن ْ ي َّع ِي ط َّهَّا ن ِهِ ق َّلاَّ ي ُّق َّز َّق ُّ ن َّي ْن َّهُّمَّا
hadiths.en:
Ja'far ibn Bashir narrated from Al -Husayn ibn Abi Al -'Ala from Abu Makhlad 
Al-Sarraj from Abu Abdullah {a.s}: Imam {a.s} ruled concerning a man who called another 
"the son of a madman," and the other replied to him, "Rather, you are the son of a 
madman."  
Imam {a.s} commanded the first man to flog his companion twenty lashes and said: 
“Know that you will also be given twenty lashes in return."  
After the first man flogged him, the flogged man was given the whip and he flogged the 
first man twenty lashes as a means of punishment to discipline both of them.  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.242 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.49 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.10 p.81 
• Al-Wafi, Vol.15 p.510 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.28 p.203 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.72 p.298  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 9, H. 5069

hadiths.chapter: 9. CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR
hadiths.ar: 5071 - وَّ ق َّالَّ إ َّمِي زُّ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِي ن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Abdullah ibn Hilal narrated from Uqbah ibn Khalid from Abu 
Abdullah {a.s} who said: I asked Imam {a.s} about a man who said to his wife, "O’ 
adulteress."  
Imam {a.s} said: “He is to be given the legal punishment and they are to be separated 
after he has been flogged, and she will no longer be his wife."  
Imam {a.s} further said: “But if he said something that slipped out unintentionally 
without having any knowledge of it, intending only to anger her, then they are not to be 
separated." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.50 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.10 p.88 • Al -Wafi, Vol.15 
p.363 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.28 p.218  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 9, H. 5070

reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR

hadiths.chapter: 9. CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR
hadiths.en:
Commander of the Faithful {a.s} said: "If there is doubt expressed with 
'perhaps' or 'it may be' in enforcing a legal punishment, then the punishment is to be 
suspended." 
[REFERENCES] Da’a’im Al-Islam, Vol.2 p.465 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.50 • Al-Wafi, Vol.15 p.553 
• Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.28 p.47 • Mustadrak Al-Wasa’il, Vol.18 p.27  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 9, H. 5071
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩، ح ٥٠٧١

hadiths.chapter: 9. CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR
hadiths.ar:
5073   -  وَّ رَّوَّى إِن ْن ُّ مَّحْن ُّوب ٍ عَّن ْ هِس َّامِ ن ْن ِ سَّالِمٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ن َّصِي زٍ ق َّالَّ: سُّن ِلَّ إ َّن ُّو عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلا مُّ عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ  
ق َّذ َّف َّ إِمْزَّإ َّي َّهُّ ن ِالز ِّن َّا وَّ هِى َّ ج َّزْسَّاءُّ صَّم َّاءُّ لأَّ ي َّسْمَّغُّ مَّا ق َّالَّ ق َّق َّال ه   ي َّس ْهَّذُّون َّ لَّهَّا عِن ْذَّ إَّلْأ ِمَّامِ ج َّلَّذَّةُّ 
إَّلْحَّذ َّ وَّ ق َّز َّق َّ ن َّي ْن َّهُّمَّا ي ُّم َّ لأَّ ي َّحِل ُّ لَّهُّ إ َّن َّذإً وَّ إ ِن ْ لَّمْ ي َّكُّن ْ لَّهَّا ن َّي ِّن َّه   ق َّهِى َّ جَّزَّإم  عَّلَّي ْهِ مَّ ا إ َّق َّامَّ مَّعَّهَّا وَّ لأَّ إ ِي ْمَّ عَّلَّي ْهَّا مِن ْه
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} said: "The one who accuses a 
foundling (laqiṭ) of fornication is subjected to the prescribed punishment ( hadd). And 
if a woman accuses her husband of fornication while he is deaf, they are separated, 
and she is never permissible for him again." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.50 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.28 p.190  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 9, H. 5072

reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩، ح ٥٠٧٢

hadiths.chapter: 9. CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR
hadiths.ar: 5074 - وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ إَّلس َّكُّون ِى ِّ إ َّن  َّ عَّلِي  اً عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال ذ َّ وَّ إ ُّلْز ِمَّ إَّلْوَّلَّذ
hadiths.en:
Ibn Mahbub narrated from Hisham ibn Salim from Abu Basir who said:  
Abu Abdullah {a.s} was asked about a man who accused his wife of adultery while she 
was mute and deaf, unable to hear what he said.  
Imam {a.s} said: “If she has witnesses who testify on her behalf before the Imam, he (the 
husband) is to be flogged with the legal punishment and they are to be separated, and 
she will never be permissible for him again. But if she has no witnesses, then she is 
forbidden to him as long as she remains with him, and there is no sin upon her from 
him." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.6 p.166 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.50 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.7 p.310 
• Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.8 p.193 • Al-Wafi, Vol.21 p.285 • Al-Wafi, Vol.22 p.962 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.22 
p.427  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 9, H. 5073

reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩، ح ٥٠٧٣

hadiths.chapter: 9. CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR
hadiths.ar:
5075  -  وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ ي ُّون ُّسَّ ن ْن ِ عَّن ْذِ إَّلز َّجْمَّن ِ عَّن ْ ن َّعْص ِ رِج َّالِهِ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال « :
ذ َّكَّزٍ إ َّوْ إ ُّن ْي َّى إِق ْي َّزَّى عَّلَّى صَّع ِي زٍ إ َّوْ كَّن ِي زٍ إ َّوْ ذ َّكَّزٍ إ َّوْ إ ُّن ْي َّى إ َّوْ مُّسْلِمٍ إ َّوْ جُّز   إ َّوْ مَّمْلُّوك ٍ ق َّعَّلَّي ْهِ جَّذ ُّ إَّلْق ِزْي َّه ِ وَّ عَّلَّى ع َّي ْزِ 
إَّلْن َّالِغ ِ جَّذ ُّ إَّلْأ َّذَّب ِ
hadiths.en:
In the narration of Al-Sakuni, Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} said:  
"Whoever acknowledges a child and then denies him is to be given the legal 
punishment and held responsible for the child." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.261 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.51 • Al-Ash’athiyat, Vol.1 p.125 • 
Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.10 p.87 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.4 p.233 • Al-Wafi, Vol.15 p.364 • Al-Wafi, Vol.23 p.1427 • 
Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.28 p.209 • Mustadrak Al-Wasa’il, Vol.17 p.214 • Mustadrak Al-Wasa’il, Vol.18 p.104  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 9, H. 5074

hadiths.chapter: 9. CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR
hadiths.ar:
5076 - وَّ ق َّالَّ عَّلِى    عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم  لأَّ عَّلَّى إَّلن  َّان ِمِ 
جَّي  َّى ي َّسْي َّي ْق ِظ
hadiths.en:
In the narration of Yunus ibn Abd Al-Rahman from some of his narrators from 
Abu Abdullah {a.s}, he said: "Every adult, whether male or female, who falsely accuses 
a minor or an adult, a male or a female, a Muslim, a free person, or a slave, is subject 
to the prescribed punishment (hadd Al-firya).  
And for one who is not yet an adult, the punishment is disciplinary ( hadd Al-adab)." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.51 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.10 p.89 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.4 
p.234 • Al-Wafi, Vol.15 p.360 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.28 p.186  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 9, H. 5075

hadiths.chapter: 9. CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR
hadiths.ar:
5077  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحَّسَّن ُّ ن ْن ُّ مَّحْن ُّوب ٍ عَّن ِ إَّلْعَّلاَّءِ وَّ إ َّن ِى  إ َّي  ُّوب َّ عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ مُّسْلِمٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْ هِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى   
رَّج ُّلٍ ق َّالَّ لأِمْزَّإ َّي ِهِ ي َّا ر َّإن ِي َّه ُّ إ َّن َّا ر َّن َّي ْث ُّ ن ِك ِ ق َّال اهَّا وَّ إ َّم َّا ق َّوْلُّهُّ إ َّن َّا ر َّن َّي ْث ُّ ن ِك ِ ق َّلاَّ جَّذ َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ 
ق ِي هِ إ ِلأ َّ إ َّن ْ ي َّس ْهَّذَّ عَّلَّى ن َّق ْسِهِ إ َّرْن َّغَّ مَّز َّإب ٍ ن ِالز ِّن َّا عِن ْذَّ إَّلْأ ِمَّامِ
hadiths.en:
Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} said:  
"There is no legal punishment upon a mad person until they regain sanity, nor upon a 
child until they reach maturity, nor upon a sleeping person until they wake up."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.51 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.10 p.152 • Al-Wafi, Vol.15 
p.282 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.28 p.22 • Al-Fusul Al-Muhimmah, Vol.2 p.512 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.76 p.88 • 
Mustadrak Al-Wasa’il, Vol.18 p.13  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 9, H. 5076

hadiths.chapter: 9. CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR
hadiths.ar:
5078  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحَّسَّن ُّ ن ْن ُّ مَّحْن ُّوب ٍ عَّن ْ ن ُّعَّي ْمِ ن ْن ِ إ ِن ْزَّإهِي مَّ عَّن ْ مِسْمَّغٍ إ َّن ِى  سَّي  َّارٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ ع لَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : 
ق ِى  إ َّرْن َّعَّه ٍ س َّهِذُّوإ عَّلَّى إِمْزَّإ َّة ٍ ن ِالْق ُّح ُّورِ إ َّجَّذُّهُّمْ ر َّوْج ُّهَّا ق َّال   وَّ ي ُّلاَّعِن ُّهَّا ر َّوْج ُّهَّا وَّ ي ُّق َّز َّق ُّ ن َّي ْن َّهُّمَّا وَّ لأَّ 
ي َّحِل ُّ لَّهُّ إ َّن َّذإً
hadiths.en:
Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Al -Ala and Abu Ayyub from Muhammad 
ibn Muslim from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} regarding a man who 
said to his wife, "O’ adulteress, I have committed adultery with you,"  
Imam {a.s} said: “He is liable for one legal punishment for accusing her. As for his 
statement, 'I have committed adultery with you,' there is no legal punishment for that 
unless he testifies against himself four times for committing adultery before the 
Imam." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.211 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.51 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.10 p.76 
• Al-Manaqib, Vol.4 p.263 • Al-Wafi, Vol.15 p.364 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.28 p.195  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 9, H. 5077

hadiths.chapter: 9. CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR
hadiths.ar:
5079 - وَّ ق َّذْ رُّوِى  
ق َّالَّ مُّصَّن ِّف ُّ هَّذ َّإ إلْكِي َّاب ِ رَّجِمَّهُّ إلل َّهُّ: هَّذ َّإن ِ إلْحَّذِي ي َّان ِ مُّي  َّق ِق َّان ِ ع َّي ْزُّ مُّح ْي َّلِق َّي ْن ِ وَّ ذ َّلِك َّ إ َّن  َّهُّ   مَّي َّى س َّهِذَّ  إ َّرْن َّعَّه   عَّلَّى إمْزَّإ َّة ٍ ن ِالْق ُّح ُّورِ 
إ َّجَّذُّهُّمْ ر َّوْج ُّهَّا وَّ لَّمْ ي َّن ْف ِ وَّلَّذَّهَّا ق َّالز  َّوْح ُّ إ َّجَّذُّ إلس  ُّهُّوذِ وَّ مَّي َّى ن َّق َّى وَّلَّذَّهَّا مَّغَّ إ ِق َّامَّه ِ إلس  َّهَّاذَّ ة ِ عَّلَّي ْهَّا ن ِالز ِّن َّا ج ُّلِذَّ إلي  َّلَّاي َّه ُّ إلْحَّذ َّ وَّ 
لَّأعَّن َّهَّا ر َّوْج ُّهَّا وَّ ق ُّزِّق َّ ن َّ ي ْن َّهُّمَّا وَّ لَّمْ ي َّحِل َّ لَّهُّ إ َّن َّذإً لِأ َّن  َّ إللِّعَّان َّ لَّأ ي َّكُّون ُّ إ ِل َّأ ن ِن َّق ْى ِ إلْوَّلَّذِ  وَّ إ ِذ َّإ ق َّذ َّف َّ عَّن ْذ  جُّز إً ج ُّلِذَّ ي َّمَّان ِي ن َّ ج َّلْذَّة ً لِأ َّن  َّ  
هَّذ َّإ مِن ْ جُّق ُّوق ِ إلن  َّاسِ .
hadiths.en:
Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Nu'aym ibn Ibrahim from Misma Abu 
Sayyar from Abu Abdullah {a.s} regarding four people who testified against a woman for 
adultery, and one of them was her husband. 
Imam {a.s} said: “The three (other witnesses) are to be flogged, and her husband is to 
perform lian (mutual cursing) with her. They are to be separated, and she will never be 
permissible for him again." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.52 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.10 p.79 • Al -Wafi, Vol.15 
p.260 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.22 p.432  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 9, H. 5078

hadiths.chapter: 9. CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR
hadiths.ar:
5080  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحَّسَّن ُّ ن ْن ُّ مَّحْن ُّوب ٍ عَّن ْ عَّن ْذِ إَّلز َّجْمَّن ِ عَّن ْ عُّن َّي ْذِ ن ْن ِ ر ُّرَّإرَّة َّ ق َّالَّ سَّمِعْث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ   عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ 
ي َّق ُّول  لَّص َّزَّن ْي ُّهُّ إَّلْحَّذ َّ جَّذ َّ إَّلْحُّزِّ إ ِلأ َّ سَّوْطاً
hadiths.en:
It has been narrated: "Indeed, the husband is considered one of the 
witnesses." 
[AL SADUQ] 
The author of this book, may Allah {SWT} have mercy on him, said:  
These two narrations are in agreement and not contradictory. This is because 
whenever four people testify against a woman for adultery, and one of them is her 
husband who does not deny his child, the husband is counted as one of the witnesses.  
However, if he denies his child while testimony is established against her for adultery, 
the three others are to be flogged with the legal punishment, and the husband must 
perform lian (mutual cursing) with her, leading to their separation, and she will never 
be permissible for him again.  
This is because lian only occurs when the child is denied.  
Additionally, if a slave accuses a free person of adultery, he is to be flogged eighty 
lashes because this falls under the rights of the people.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.52 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.22 p.433  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 9, H. 5079

reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR

hadiths.chapter: 9. CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR
hadiths.ar:
5081  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحَّسَّن ُّ ن ْن ُّ مَّحْن ُّوب ٍ عَّن ْ جَّم َّاذِ ن ْن ِ ر ِي َّاذٍ عَّن ْ سُّلَّي ْمَّان َّ ن ْن ِ ج َّالِذٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْ هِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ:  
« إَّلْحُّزِّ ي َّمَّان ِي ن َّ ج َّلْذَّة ً إ َّذ َّى مِن ْ مُّكَّاي َّن َّي ِهِ 
س َّي ْن اً إ َّوْ لَّمْ ي ُّو َّذ جَّق  ُّ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ ي ُّطْزَّحُّ 
عَّن ْهُّ ج َّمْسُّون َّ ج َّلْذَّة ً وَّ ي ُّص ْزَّ ب ُّ ج َّمْسِي ن
hadiths.en:
Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Abd Al-Rahman from Ubayd ibn Zurarah 
who said: I heard Abu Abdullah {a.s} say, "If a man were brought to me who had accused 
a Muslim slave of adultery, and we knew nothing about the slave except goodness, I 
would administer the legal punishment upon him - the same punishment as for a free 
person, except one lash less." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.208 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.52 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.10 p.71 
• Al-Wafi, Vol.15 p.373 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.28 p.178 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Vol.3 p.575 • Tafsir Kanz 
Al-Daqaiq, Vol.9 p.248  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 9, H. 5080

hadiths.chapter: 9. CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR
hadiths.ar:
5082  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إِن ْن ُّ مَّحْن ُّوب ٍ عَّن ْ مَّالِك ِ ن ْن ِ عَّطِي  َّه َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ن َّصِي زٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ ي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  
ق ِى  إِمْزَّإ َّة ٍ ق َّذ َّق َّث ْ رَّج ُّلاً ق َّال
hadiths.en:
Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Hammad ibn Ziyad from Sulayman ibn 
Khalid from Abu Abdullah {a.s} who said:  
"Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} was asked about a mukatab (a slave who has a contract for 
freedom) who falsely accused a Muslim man.  
Imam {a.s} said: ‘He is to be flogged with the punishment of a free man - eighty lashes - 
whether he has paid any part of his contract or not.'  
It was asked: ‘What if he commits adultery while he is a mukatab and has not paid 
anything from his contract?'  
Imam {a.s} said: ‘This is the right of Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic. Fifty lashes are 
waived from him, and he is to be flogged fifty lashes.'" 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.236 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.52 • Al-Wafi, Vol.15 p.376  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 9, H. 5081

reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩، ح ٥٠٨١

hadiths.chapter: 9. CHAPTER ON THE LEGAL PUNISHMENT FOR
hadiths.ar:
5083 - وَّ رَّوَّى مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ سِن َّان ٍ عَّن ِ إَّلْعَّلاَّءِ ن ْن ِ إَّلْق ُّص َّي ْلِ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ: ق ُّلْ ث ُّ لَّهُّ إَّلز َّج ُّلُّ ي َّن ْي َّق ِى  
مِن ْ وَّلَّذِةِ وَّ ق َّذْ إ َّق َّز َّ ن ِهِ ق َّال ي ن َّ سَّوْطاً جَّذ َّ إَّلْمَّمْلُّوك ِ وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ مِن ْ إ َّمَّه ٍ ق َّلاَّ 
س َّى ْءَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ  وَّ إ ِذ َّإ ق َّالَّ رَّج ُّل  لِزَّج ُّلٍ إ ِن  َّك َّ ي َّعْمَّلُّ عَّمَّلَّ ق َّوْمِ لُّوطٍ ي َّن ْكِحُّ إَّلزِّج َّالَّ ص ُّزِب َّ ي َّمَّان ِي ن َّ ج َّلْذَّة ً وَّ كَّذ َّلِ ك َّ إ ِن ْ ق َّالَّ  
لَّهُّ ي َّا مَّعْق ُّوح ُّ ي َّا مَّن ْكُّو حُّ ج ُّلِذَّ جَّذ َّ إَّلْق َّاذ ِف ِ ي َّمَّان ِي ن َّ ج َّلْذَّة ً وَّ إ ِن ْ ق َّذ َّف َّ رَّج ُّل  ق َّوْماً ن ِكَّلِمَّه ٍ وَّإجِذَّة ٍ ق َّعَّلَّي ْهِ جَّذ   وَّإجِذ  إ ِ ذ َّإ لَّمْ 
ي ُّسَّمِّهِمْ ن ِا َّسْمَّان ِهِمْ وَّ إ ِن ْ سَّم َّاهُّمْ ق َّعَّلَّي ْهِ لِكُّلِّ رَّج ُّلٍ سَّم َّاةُّ جَّذ   رَّوَّى ذ َّلِك َّ ن ُّزَّي ْذ  إَّلْعِح ْلِى  ُّ  عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ.  
وَّ رُّوِى ح ْي َّمِعِي ن َّ ص ُّزِب َّ جَّذ إً وَّإجِذإً  
وَّ إ ِن ْ ق َّذ َّف َّ رَّج ُّل  رَّج ُّلاً ق َّح ُّلِذَّ ي ُّم َّ عَّاذَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ ن ِالْق َّذ ْف ِ ق َّا ِن ْ كَّان َّ ق َّالَّ إ ِن  َّ   إَّل َّذ ِى  ق ُّلْث ُّ لَّك َّ جَّق    لَّمْ ي ُّح ْلَّذْ وَّ إ ِن ْ ق َّذ َّق َّهُّ ن ِالز ِّن َّا  
ن َّعْذَّ مَّا ج ُّلِذَّ ق َّعَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلْحَّذ ُّ وَّ إ ِن ْ ق َّذ َّق َّهُّ ق َّن ْلَّ إ َّن ْ ي ُّح ْلَّذَّ ن ِعَّس ْزِ ق َّذ َّق َّاب ٍ لَّمْ ي َّكُّن ْ عَّلَّي ْهِ إ ِلأ َّ جَّ ذ   وَّإجِذ
hadiths.en:
Ibn Mahbub narrated from Malik ibn Atiyyah from Abu Basir from Abu 
Abdullah {a.s} regarding a woman who accused a man (of adultery). 
Imam {a.s} said: “She is to be flogged eighty lashes." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.205 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.53 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.10 p.65 
• Al-Wafi, Vol.15 p.358 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.28 p.175 • Tafsir Al-Burhan, Vol.4 p.48  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 9, H. 5082
